English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Kramer family papers
Kramer family.
3970
5.5 cubic feet. 5.5 cubic feet.
1939-2009. Correspondence, pictures, diaries, and other papers. Papers include letters written to the Kramer family from Kevin Kramer while in Vienna. Also, pictures, letters, and diaries relating to Jeffrey A. Kramer's service aboard the U.S.S. Nimitz; papers concerning Samuel Kramer's World War II service; and Anne Gudis' and Samuel's war-time correspondence. Also, papers, research materials, notes, clippings, and correspondence relating to the history of the Ithaca Jewish Community. Collection material in English 2070362
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
Sept. 1986; rev. May 1991; rev. Oct. 1991; addl. Oct. 1993; addl. June 1995
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, Aug. 2002
Date modified:
Ben Wrubel, October 2018
Cite As:
Kramer family papers, #3970. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Samuel Kramer of Ithaca, New York married Anne Gudis of Newark, New Jersey. Sam served in the United States Army during World War II. In 2009, he was recognized for his wartime service by being named a Chevalier of the Legion of Honor by the French government. Sam and Anne had two sons, Kevin and Jeffrey, and a daughter, Michele. Kevin attended Cornell University and the Institute of European Studies in Vienna, Austria. Jeffrey earned a Master's Degree in Hospital Administration from Cornell University in 1973. He served aboard the U.S.S. Nimitz at the time of the aborted rescue mission of the U.S. hostages in Iran in 1980.
Scope and content
The collection contains papers from the Kramer family dating 1939-2009. It contains correspondence and greeting cards, including letters written to the Kramer family from Kevin Kramer while in Vienna; pictures, letters, and diaries relating to Jeffrey A. Kramer's service aboard the U.S.S. Nimitz; papers concerning Samuel Kramer's World War II service and his selection to the French Legion of Honor, including his 2009 certificate from the Legion; and Anne Gudis' and Samuel's war-time correspondence. The collection also includes papers, research materials, notes, clippings, and correspondence relating to the history of the Ithaca Jewish community.
Photographs. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs. Families -- Virginia -- Virginia Beach. World War, 1939-1945. Jews -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Iran Hostage Crisis, 1979-1981 -- Personal narratives. Families -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Kramer, Anne G. Kramer, Jeffrey A. Kramer, Kevin Leslie. Kramer, Samuel J. Cramer family Nimitz (Ship)
